info.title: Nocturn
info.description:
**Nocturn** — AI-powered note-taking application with semantic search
and an intelligent assistant that can create, edit, and organize your notes.

## Core concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Note** | Markdown document with title, content, version counter, and tags. Supports soft-delete (trash) and restore. |
| **Tag** | User-scoped label attached to notes for filtering and organization. |
| **AI Session** | Chat conversation with the AI assistant. Each session holds an ordered list of messages. |
| **Proposal** | An action the AI suggests (edit/create/delete a note, add/remove tags). The user can **apply** or **dismiss** each proposal. |
| **Pending Confirmation** | A bulk operation (e.g. "add tag X to 10 notes") that requires explicit user confirmation before execution. |

## Authentication

All endpoints except `/api/auth/register`, `/api/auth/login`, and `/api/health`
require a valid JWT access token in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.

- **Access token** — short-lived JWT returned by `POST /api/auth/login`.
- **Refresh token** — long-lived, stored in an httponly cookie (`path=/api/auth`).
  Use `POST /api/auth/refresh` to obtain a new access token.

## AI streaming protocol

`POST /api/ai/sessions/{id}/messages` returns an **SSE stream** (`text/event-stream`).

Each SSE frame has the format:
```
event: <type>
data: <json>
```

Event types:

| Event | Payload | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `ai:text_delta` | `{"delta": "..."}` | Incremental text chunk from the assistant |
| `ai:proposal` | `{Proposal}` | Proposed note action |
| `ai:pending_confirmation` | `{PendingConfirmation}` | Bulk operation awaiting user confirmation |
| `ai:error` | `{"code": "...", "message": "..."}` | Error during generation |
| `ai:done` | `{"message": {Message}}` | Stream complete with the final saved message |

## Optimistic concurrency

Note updates use version-based concurrency control.
The client must send the current `version` — if it doesn't match the server's
version, a `409 Conflict` is returned.

## Semantic search (RAG)

Notes are chunked and embedded asynchronously by a background worker.
`POST /api/rag/search` performs cosine-similarity search over embeddings.
Newly created/edited notes may take up to 30 seconds to become searchable.

post.description:
Create a new user account.

A confirmation email is sent to the provided address.
The account is created immediately but `is_email_confirmed`
remains `false` until the user calls `POST /confirm-email`
with the token from the email.

Returns a human-readable status message.

Authenticate with email and password.

On success returns a short-lived JWT `access_token` in the JSON body
and sets an **httponly** `refresh_token` cookie
(path `/api/auth`, secure, samesite=strict).

The access token should be sent in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
for all subsequent authenticated requests.

Issue a new access token using the refresh token from the httponly cookie.

Also rotates the refresh token itself (the old one becomes invalid).
No request body is needed — the refresh token is read from the cookie
set during login.

Invalidate the current refresh token and clear the cookie.

Safe to call even if no refresh token is present (idempotent).
The access token is **not** revoked server-side — it simply expires.

Confirm the user's email address using the token received via email.

Sets `is_email_confirmed = true` on the user account.
The token is single-use and time-limited.

Send a password-reset email to the given address.

Always returns a success message regardless of whether the email
exists in the system (to prevent user enumeration).

Set a new password using the reset token from the email.

The token is single-use. After a successful reset the user must
log in again with the new password.

Resend the email-confirmation message.

No-op if the email is already confirmed or does not exist
(to prevent user enumeration).

get.description:
Return the profile of the currently authenticated user.

Requires a valid `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` header.

put.description:
Update the current user's display nickname.

The nickname must be 2–32 characters long. Returns the full
updated user profile.

Change the current user's password.

Requires the current password for verification. The new password
must be 8–128 characters. Existing sessions remain valid —
the user is not logged out.

post.description:
Permanently delete the current user's account and all associated data.

Requires the account password for confirmation.
This action is **irreversible** — all notes, tags, chat sessions,
and embeddings are removed.

Create a new note with optional title, content, and tags.

The note is created with `version = 1`. If `tag_ids` are provided,
they are linked to the note immediately (maximum 10 tags per note).

Returns the full note object including generated `id` and timestamps.

get.description:
Return a paginated list of the user's notes.

By default only active (non-deleted) notes are returned, sorted by
`updated_at` descending. Pass `deleted=true` to list trashed notes.

Supports full-text search via `search` and tag-based filtering via
`tag_ids` (comma-separated UUIDs — only notes having **all** listed
tags are returned).

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max number of notes to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of notes to return

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Full-text search query (matches title and content)
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Full-text search query (matches title and content)

parameters.name: tag_ids

schema.description: Comma-separated tag UUIDs to filter by (AND logic)
schema.title: Tag Ids

parameters.description: Comma-separated tag UUIDs to filter by (AND logic)

get.description:
Search active notes by keywords.

Pass one or more comma-separated keywords. A note is returned only when
**every** keyword appears in its title or content (case-insensitive).
Results are sorted by `updated_at` descending.

parameters.name: keywords

schema.description: Comma-separated keywords (AND logic — note must match all of them)
schema.title: Keywords

parameters.description: Comma-separated keywords (AND logic — note must match all of them)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max number of notes to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of notes to return

get.description:
Return the full note object including content, tags, and version.

Works for both active and soft-deleted notes.

404.description: Note not found or does not belong to user

put.description:
Update a note's title and/or content.

Uses **optimistic concurrency control**: the client must send the
current `version` of the note. If the server's version differs,
a `409 Conflict` is returned and the client should re-fetch the
note before retrying.

On success the `version` is incremented by one.
Only fields present in the request body are updated (partial update).

409.description: Version conflict — the note was modified by another request

delete.description:
Delete a note.

**Soft-delete** (default): sets `deleted_at` timestamp.
The note can later be restored via `POST /{note_id}/restore`.

**Permanent delete** (`permanent=true`): irreversibly removes the
note and all associated data (embeddings, tag links).

parameters.name: permanent

schema.description: If `true`, permanently remove the note. Otherwise move it to trash (soft-delete).
schema.title: Permanent

parameters.description: If `true`, permanently remove the note. Otherwise move it to trash (soft-delete).

post.description:
Restore a soft-deleted note from trash.

Clears the `deleted_at` timestamp and returns the restored note.
Has no effect if the note was permanently deleted.

put.description:
Replace the note's tag set with the provided list.

Pass an empty `tag_ids` array to remove all tags.
Maximum 10 tags per note.

Returns the updated note with the new `tags` list.

post.description:
Fetch multiple notes by their IDs in a single request.

Useful for resolving `attached_note_ids` from chat messages.
Notes that don't exist or belong to another user are silently
skipped. Maximum 50 IDs per request.

Create a new tag for the current user.

Tag names are unique per user (case-insensitive).
Tags can later be attached to notes via `PUT /api/notes/{note_id}/tags`.

409.description: Tag with this name already exists

get.description:
Return a paginated list of the user's tags.

Supports optional substring search by tag name.
Results are sorted alphabetically.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max number of tags to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of tags to return

get.description: Return a single tag by ID.

404.description: Tag not found or does not belong to user

put.description:
Change the name of an existing tag.

The new name must be unique among the user's tags.
All notes linked to this tag keep the association.

409.description: Another tag with this name already exists

delete.description:
Delete a tag and remove it from all associated notes.

This is a permanent operation — there is no trash/restore for tags.

post.description:
Search the user's notes using semantic (vector) similarity.

The query is embedded and compared against pre-computed note chunk
embeddings using cosine similarity. Only the current user's notes
are searched.

Returns up to `limit` results ordered by relevance score (highest first).
Each result contains the matching chunk text and its parent `note_id`,
which can be used to fetch the full note.

**Note:** embeddings are computed asynchronously by the background
worker. Newly created or recently edited notes may take up to 30
seconds to become searchable.

Create a new AI chat session.

Optionally pass `dismiss_session_id` to dismiss (soft-delete) a
previous session in the same request — useful for "New chat" flows
where the old empty session should be cleaned up.

The session is created with `title = null`; it is auto-titled after
the first user message (first 100 characters).

get.description:
Return a paginated list of the user's chat sessions.

Sorted by `last_message_at` descending (most recent first).

delete.description: Permanently delete a session and all its messages.

get.description:
Return paginated messages for a session.

Messages are sorted chronologically (oldest first).
Each assistant message may include an `actions` array with
proposals and pending confirmations.

post.description:
Send a user message and stream the AI assistant's response via SSE.

**Pre-validation** runs before the stream starts and may return a
regular JSON error response (400/404/409) — the client should check
the `Content-Type` header to distinguish JSON from SSE.

**SSE event types** (each `data` payload is JSON):

| Event | Payload | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ai:text_delta` | `{"delta": "..."}` | Incremental text chunk |
| `ai:proposal` | `{Proposal object}` | A proposed note action (edit/create/delete/add_tags/remove_tags) |
| `ai:pending_confirmation` | `{PendingConfirmation}` | Bulk operation awaiting user confirmation |
| `ai:error` | `{"code": "...", "message": "..."}` | Error during generation (e.g. `"conflict"`) |
| `ai:done` | `{"message": {Message}}` | Stream complete — contains the full saved assistant message |

**Concurrency**: only one generation per session is allowed at a time.
A Redis lock (`generating:{session_id}`) enforces this — a second
request returns `409`.

**Cancellation**: call `POST /cancel` to abort a running generation.

**Attached notes**: up to 5 note IDs can be attached. Their content
is included in the LLM context so the AI can reference them.

400.description: Validation error (empty content, too many attachments, etc.)

409.description: Another generation is already in progress for this session

post.description:
Signal the server to stop an ongoing AI generation for this session.

Sets a Redis key (`cancel:{session_id}`) which is polled between
tool-calling rounds. The active SSE stream will end with an
`ai:done` event shortly after.

Safe to call even if no generation is in progress (no-op).

patch.description:
Update the status of a single AI proposal.

**Status transitions:**
- `"applied"` — execute the proposed action (edit note, create note,
  delete note, add/remove tags). The action is carried out immediately
  and the status changes to `applied`.
- `"dismissed"` — reject the proposal without executing it.

Returns the full updated message (with all actions in their current state).

Each proposal can only be finalized once — attempting to change an
already-applied or already-dismissed proposal returns `400`.

404.description: Session, message, or action not found

400.description: Action already finalized or invalid status transition

post.description:
Confirm a pending bulk operation and stream back the individual proposals.

When the AI suggests a bulk action (e.g. "add tag X to 10 notes"),
it creates a `pending_confirmation` action. The user can either
confirm (this endpoint) or dismiss (`POST /dismiss`).

On confirmation, the server processes each note and streams back
individual `ai:proposal` events via SSE, followed by `ai:done`
with the updated message.

200.description: SSE event stream with individual proposals

post.description:
Dismiss a pending bulk operation without executing it.

Sets the confirmation status to `dismissed` and returns
the updated message.

get.description:
Return a paginated list of all users in the system.

**Admin only.** Supports optional search by email or nickname substring.
Return a single user's details by ID. **Admin only.**

put.description:
Set a user's `is_active` flag. **Admin only.**

Deactivated users cannot log in or use the API.
An admin cannot deactivate their own account.

403.description: Not an admin or trying to deactivate yourself

put.description:
Change a user's role to `user` or `admin`. **Admin only.**

An admin cannot change their own role.

403.description: Not an admin or trying to change own role

get.description:
Returns `{"status": "ok"}` if the API server is running.

Does not check database or Redis connectivity — use this endpoint
only for basic liveness probes.

note_ids.title: Note Ids
note_ids.description: List of note IDs to fetch (max 50)

BatchGetNotesRequest.title: BatchGetNotesRequest
BatchGetNotesRequest.description: Fetch multiple notes in one request.

items.title: Items
items.description: Requested notes (missing/inaccessible IDs are silently skipped)

ChangePasswordRequest.title: ChangePasswordRequest
ChangePasswordRequest.description: Payload for changing the account password.

token.title: Token
token.description: Single-use confirmation token from the email

CreateNoteRequest.title: CreateNoteRequest
CreateNoteRequest.description: Payload for creating a new note.

dismiss_session_id.title: Dismiss Session Id
dismiss_session_id.description: Optional: ID of a previous session to dismiss (soft-delete) atomically. Useful for 'New chat' flows.

CreateSessionRequest.title: CreateSessionRequest
CreateSessionRequest.description: Payload for creating a new chat session.

name.title: Name
name.description: Tag name (1–50 chars, unique per user)

CreateTagRequest.title: CreateTagRequest
CreateTagRequest.description: Payload for creating a new tag.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of messages in the session

MessagesListResponse.title: MessagesListResponse
MessagesListResponse.description: Paginated list of messages in a session.

NoteListItem.title: NoteListItem
NoteListItem.description: Lightweight note representation for list views.

version.title: Version
version.description: Monotonically increasing version number for optimistic concurrency

NoteSearchResponse.title: NoteSearchResponse
NoteSearchResponse.description: Result of a keyword search over notes.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address to send the reset link to

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address to resend confirmation to

token.title: Token
token.description: Single-use reset token from the email

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Max number of results to return (1–20)

results.title: Results
results.description: Matching chunks ordered by relevance (highest score first)

chunk_id.title: Chunk Id
chunk_id.description: ID of the matching embedding chunk

note_id.title: Note Id
note_id.description: Parent note ID (use to fetch the full note)

content.title: Content
content.description: Text content of the matching chunk

attached_note_ids.title: Attached Note Ids
attached_note_ids.description: Note IDs to attach as context for the AI (max 5). Their content is included in the LLM prompt.

SendMessageRequest.title: SendMessageRequest
SendMessageRequest.description: User message to send to the AI assistant.

title.title: Title
title.description: Session title (auto-set from first message, or `null`)

last_message_at.title: Last Message At
last_message_at.description: Timestamp of the last message in this session, or `null`

SetActiveRequest.title: SetActiveRequest
SetActiveRequest.description: Enable or disable a user account.

TagBrief.title: TagBrief
TagBrief.description: Minimal tag representation attached to a note.

access_token.title: Access Token
access_token.description: Short-lived JWT token for `Authorization: Bearer` header

status.title: Status
status.description: New status: `applied` (execute the action) or `dismissed` (reject it)

UpdateActionRequest.title: UpdateActionRequest
UpdateActionRequest.description: Apply or dismiss a single AI proposal.

UpdateNicknameRequest.title: UpdateNicknameRequest
UpdateNicknameRequest.description: Payload for changing the display nickname.

version.title: Version
version.description: Current note version for optimistic concurrency. Must match the server's version or 409 is returned.

UpdateNoteRequest.title: UpdateNoteRequest
UpdateNoteRequest.description: Payload for updating a note. Only provided fields are changed.

tag_ids.title: Tag Ids
tag_ids.description: New tag IDs (replaces all existing). Pass `[]` to remove all tags.

UpdateNoteTagsRequest.title: UpdateNoteTagsRequest
UpdateNoteTagsRequest.description: Replace the note's entire tag set.

UpdateSessionRequest.title: UpdateSessionRequest
UpdateSessionRequest.description: Payload for renaming a chat session.

name.title: Name
name.description: New tag name (1–50 chars, must be unique)

is_email_confirmed.title: Is Email Confirmed
is_email_confirmed.description: Whether the email has been confirmed

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the account is active (can log in)

actions.title: Actions
actions.description: List of AI actions attached to an assistant message. Each action is either a **proposal** (`type: 'proposal'`) with fields: `id`, `proposal_type` (edit_note/create_note/delete_note/add_tags/remove_tags), `status` (pending/applied/dismissed), `note_id`, `data`, `summary`; or a **pending_confirmation** (`type: 'pending_confirmation'`) with fields: `id`, `status` (pending/confirmed/dismissed), `operation_type`, `note_ids`, `params`, `summary`. `null` for user messages.

attached_note_ids.title: Attached Note Ids
attached_note_ids.description: Note IDs that were attached to a user message as context. `null` if none.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Registration, login, JWT tokens, email confirmation, password reset.

tags.name: profile
tags.description: Current user profile management — nickname, password, account deletion.

tags.name: notes
tags.description: CRUD for markdown notes with tags, soft-delete/restore, and batch operations.

tags.name: tags
tags.description: User-scoped tags for note organization and filtering.

tags.name: ai
tags.description: AI chat sessions, SSE message streaming, proposals, and bulk confirmations.

tags.name: admin
tags.description: User management for administrators — list users, change roles, enable/disable accounts.
